Seo.siteTitle: Lorenzo Oliva — Full Stack Web Developer & Artist
Seo.siteDescription: Lorenzo Oliva, full stack web developer (React, Next.js, Node.js, TypeScript) and artist. Portfolio of development projects and artworks.
Seo.ogImageAlt: Lorenzo Oliva — Full Stack Web Developer & Artist

Layout.footerLabel1: No part of this site or its content may be reproduced, copied, modified, or adapted without the written permission of the rightful owner.

Home.presentationTxt1: I am a web developer specializing in frontend, an artist, with experience in decoration and the use of digital graphic software. I graduated from art school and made several approaches to the tech world, until in 2024 I started my training in development.
Home.presentationTxt2: From this webpage, you have access to my two worlds: art and development.
Home.modalHelloLabel: Hi, I’m Lorenzo, nice to meet you!

ArtSection.galleryLead: My work centres on painting — mainly oil and mixed media — through which I explore the human soul, nature, time and existence, with an evocative use of colour and light.
ArtSection.galleryStatement: In my view, art should not stop at mere representation but portray the essence of its creator, reconciling ethics with aesthetics. Drawing on all my knowledge, through painting I lay out my thought: not concise stories, but universal concepts rooted in my vision of human life. My aim is to look towards the future of art with works turned to the contemporary, while following in the footsteps of the past. Every work is like a dream through which the artist reveals himself.

DevSection.followCode: Visit my GitHub profile and view the code
DevSection.followProject: Follow the link and try the project

Freedihare.heroLead: Freedihare is built for people: it brings awareness to eating through sharing and communication, giving everyone back the freedom in their own way of eating.
Freedihare.heroIntro: It is a desktop application to plan, log and analyse your nutrition day by day. You log your meals and the app automatically computes calories, macros, daily balance and trends over time: no technical skills required.
Freedihare.overviewLead: The app is organised into five areas, reachable from the sidebar or from the bottom bar on small screens.
Freedihare.dashboardText: The main screen shows the current day and lets you log meals in seconds: weekly calendar, the day's meals with their macros, a real-time calorie balance with a circular chart, weight and measurements with BMI and body fat computed automatically. You can import a meal from your favourites or copy a whole day from a previous date.
Freedihare.diaryText: The Diary is for looking back: review past days, compare periods and discover your trends. Pick the range (week, month, year), filter by nutritional phase and read the charts: expected versus consumed calories, weight trend, BMI, macro composition.
Freedihare.foodsText: The catalogue of foods and recipes you use to build meals, organised by brands, shops and categories. Each food has values per 100g and typical portions; each recipe computes total macros from its ingredients and can be used as an ingredient of another, with cascading recalculation.
Freedihare.mealsText: The food combinations you use often (your everyday breakfast, the post-workout snack) saved once and reloaded with a click, without re-adding the ingredients every time.
Freedihare.profileText: It gathers personal data and computed values (basal metabolism, daily needs, BMI, body fat percentage), measurements over time and, above all, nutritional phases: periods with defined macro goals, with activity level, dates and a calorie delta. From then on the Dashboard compares your intake against the active phase's goals.
Freedihare.sharingText: You can link your account to another person's: a partner, a family member, a nutritionist. The link is mutual: you share foods, recipes and cookware, and you can copy a whole day from the other's diary. Weight, measurements, phases, diary and favourite meals always stay private.
Freedihare.macrosText: Freedihare keeps eight nutritional values under control for every food, recipe and meal. The same colours appear throughout the app: in meal badges, in charts and in warnings when a value exceeds the phase goal.
Freedihare.dayMorning: Morning · weigh yourself fasting and log breakfast, importing it from favourites if it is the usual one.
Freedihare.dayLunch: Lunch and snack · add the meals and enter the weights: the remaining macros update instantly.
Freedihare.dayDinner: Dinner · check the calorie chart and close the day.
Freedihare.dayTrend: Over time · switch to the Diary to see how you are doing across week, month or year.
Freedihare.closingText: Freedihare is a personal project, designed and built end-to-end: from the data model and the APIs through to the desktop interface. It is a structured MVP, currently in testing and set to be expanded.
